title: Construction of eigenvarieties and coherent cohomology
speaker: Vincent Pilloni

proof_stages.label: Coherent-cohomology eigenvariety program
proof_stages.summary: The talk starts from the Hecke action on H^0 of coherent automorphic sheaves on a PEL Shimura variety and organizes the eigenvariety construction into p-adic interpolation of sheaves, Coleman spectral theory, and a small-slope classicality theorem.

proof_stages.label: Modular-curve interpolation obstruction
proof_stages.summary: In the modular-curve model, classical omega^k is recast as invariant functions on the rigid differential torsor, but naive kappa-homogeneous functions on the punctured unit ball vanish for nonintegral p-adic weights, forcing the construction to use smaller subdomains.

proof_stages.label: Canonical subgroups and the Hodge-Tate map
proof_stages.summary: The Hodge-Tate map for finite flat group schemes and canonical-subgroup theorems for sufficiently ordinary abelian schemes provide controlled finite subgroups whose Hodge-Tate images approximate the conormal sheaf.

proof_stages.label: Hodge-Tate neighborhoods inside the differential torsor
proof_stages.summary: For strict neighborhoods X(v) of the ordinary locus, the Hodge-Tate images of generators of the dual canonical subgroup cut out domains F_n in the differential torsor, forming controlled unions of small balls with compatible Z_p^*-action.

proof_stages.label: Overconvergent sheaves and forms on modular curves
proof_stages.summary: For a p-adic weight kappa and large enough n, kappa-invariant functions on F_n define an invertible sheaf omega_kappa independent of n, whose sections over shrinking neighborhoods give overconvergent modular forms varying analytically in kappa and admitting an integral moduli description.

proof_stages.label: Siegel and PEL Banach-sheaf generalization
proof_stages.summary: On Siegel varieties, the weight-dependent ranks of classical GL_g automorphic sheaves are bypassed by embedding them into interpolating Banach sheaves built from locally analytic induction to an Iwahori subgroup, yielding analytic variation and an exact classical-subobject sequence.

proof_stages.label: Local eigenvariety and classicality theorem
proof_stages.summary: Starting from a cuspidal finite-slope Hecke eigenform, Coleman theory applied to the Banach sheaves produces a finite equidimensional rigid space over weight space with a universal eigencharacter, and explicit small-slope inequalities ensure that suitable specializations are classical forms.

proof_stages.label: Comparison and range of validity
proof_stages.summary: The coherent construction is compared with cohomological eigenvarieties and its scope is described in terms of canonical-subgroup existence and finite-slope classicality for split type A/C PEL Shimura varieties and certain Hilbert modular cases.
